title: Add Starting Equipment for New Characters & Clean Up Drop Rate UI

requirements.text: When a new character is created, automatically grant them a starter equipment set consisting of at minimum one Weapon and one Armor piece. The starter weapon must provide enough base physical damage so that the character deals meaningful damage to level 1 monsters (well above 1 damage). The starter armor must provide enough base defense to meaningfully reduce incoming damage from level 1 monsters (which currently deal ~13 damage). Generate these items using the existing lootGenerator.ts item generation logic at item level 1 with Common rarity, then immediately equip them to the character's weapon and armor slots. This must happen at character creation time so the player never starts a dungeon run unequipped.

quotes: My character does 1 damage and the level 1 monster does 13, and my starting character only has 20 life. Lets add basic equipment when you create a character.

acceptanceCriteria: A newly created character has a weapon equipped in their weapon slot from the moment the character sheet is first shown.
A newly created character has an armor piece equipped in their armor slot from the moment the character sheet is first shown.
The starter weapon provides sufficient base physical damage so that level 1 combat results in more than 1 damage per hit against a level 1 monster.
The starter armor provides sufficient base defense so that level 1 monster attacks deal noticeably less than 13 damage to the player.
Starter items are generated at item level 1 with Common rarity via the existing lootGenerator logic.
The starter items appear in the character's equipped slots (not just inventory) immediately after character creation.

requirements.text: In DungeonSelectScreen.tsx, remove the non-functional drop rate percentage scroller/slider UI element. Replace it with a clean static informational display showing the current effective XP rate and drop rate for the selected monster level, calculated using the existing flat 6% base drop rate logic (6% base, reduced by 1% per level the monster is below the player's level, flooring at 0%). The display should be read-only text, matching the cleaner informational style used in prior versions of the screen.

quotes: The drop % scroller doesn't change, it's just a static 6%. Might as well remove it and just show the experience/drop % like the prior version.

acceptanceCriteria: The drop rate scroller/slider is completely removed from DungeonSelectScreen.tsx.
A static text display shows the current calculated drop rate based on the chosen monster level and player level.
The displayed drop rate reflects the flat 6% base minus 1% per level the monster is below the player (e.g., same level = 6%, 3 levels below = 3%, 6+ levels below = 0%).
XP rate information is also displayed statically alongside the drop rate.
No interactive input element exists for modifying the drop rate — it is purely informational.

constraints: Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
Starter equipment generation must use the existing lootGenerator.ts logic — do not introduce a separate item creation pathway.
The backend character record must store the starter equipment in the same equipped slots structure used by all other equipment.
nonGoals: Changing the drop rate formula itself (it remains 6% base minus 1% per level gap).
Adding starter abilities or consumables — only weapon and armor.
Rebalancing monster stats or player base stats beyond what the starter equipment provides.
